gloss.en: a feeling of great sadness

gloss.en: a type or group of similar things

gloss.en: having a sharp taste like a lemon

gloss.en: in or toward the direction between south and west

gloss.en: small bright flashes of light

gloss.en: to focus on one subject or area

gloss.en: for a particular purpose or reason

gloss.en: having a strong, hot taste from spices

gloss.en: shaped like a curve winding around a center

gloss.en: said in speech rather than writing

gloss.en: a soft material that absorbs water

gloss.en: relating to games and physical activities

gloss.en: a piece of information shown in numbers

gloss.en: a period of staying somewhere; a visit

gloss.en: a small label that you stick on something

gloss.en: not moving; calm and quiet

gloss.en: with strong winds and rain

gloss.en: a person who tells stories

gloss.en: in an unusual or surprising way

gloss.en: a person that you do not know

gloss.en: a thin tube for drinking through

gloss.en: a small soft red summer fruit

gloss.en: the quality of being physically strong

gloss.en: worry caused by problems or too much to do

gloss.en: feeling worried and unable to relax

gloss.en: causing a lot of worry and pressure

gloss.en: badly affected by trouble or illness

gloss.en: thin cord used for tying things

gloss.en: a long narrow band of color

gloss.en: in a firm and serious way

words.example: I strongly believe that he is right.

gloss.en: refusing to change your mind

words.example: He is too stubborn to say sorry.

gloss.en: unable to move or make progress

gloss.en: a room where programs or music are recorded

gloss.en: filled tightly with something

words.example: The bag was stuffed with old clothes.

gloss.en: showing a lack of good judgement

words.example: It was a stupid mistake to make.

gloss.en: fashionable and attractive in design

words.example: She wore a stylish black coat.

gloss.en: present in the mind but not fully aware

gloss.en: a ship that can travel under water

gloss.en: in a way that achieves the intended aim

gloss.en: happening quickly and unexpectedly

gloss.en: quickly and unexpectedly

words.example: I suddenly realized what I had to do.

gloss.en: to be badly affected by pain or trouble

words.example: I hate to see animals suffer.

gloss.en: letters added to the end of a word

gloss.en: a jacket and trousers made of the same cloth

gloss.en: in a way that causes surprise

words.example: The plane shook a little during takeoff.

gloss.en: a natural ability to do something well

words.example: She has a real talent for music.

gloss.en: having a natural ability to do well

gloss.en: a speech or lecture on a subject

gloss.en: liking to talk a lot

words.example: My sister is friendly and talkative.

gloss.en: a narrow sticky strip for joining things

gloss.en: a member of the same team as you

gloss.en: a tendency to become angry easily

gloss.en: to be likely to do something usually
